What's Actually Happening

This Is Not a Willpower Problem

In Kigali, where the highland heat and Rwanda's extraordinary transformation pressure, the gut is often the first system to break down — and the last one anyone looks at.

  • Bloating after every meal — regardless of what you eat
  • Inconsistent digestion — constipation alternating with urgency
  • Brain fog, low energy, and mood crashes after eating
  • Food sensitivities that seem to multiply over time

Your gut is not just sensitive. It is inflamed, imbalanced, and signaling that something upstream broke down.

Dr. Felicia Stoler

Clinical Assessment

What BLEU Sees

BLEU · ALVAI Protocol Engine · Kigali
Current State
Gut dysbiosis with mucosal inflammation and impaired barrier function.
Root Cause Analysis
  • Microbiome imbalance — beneficial bacteria depleted, pathogenic overgrowth
  • Intestinal hyperpermeability (leaky gut) — triggering systemic inflammation
  • Digestive enzyme deficiency — food not being broken down properly
Protocol Components
  • Spore-based probiotic (Bacillus coagulans + Lactobacillus blend)
  • Digestive enzymes (broad-spectrum, with meals)
  • L-Glutamine (mucosal repair)
  • Elimination of top 5 inflammatory triggers
  • Prebiotic fiber protocol
The Protocol

Gut Reset Protocol

30 days
01
Spore-Based Probiotic
Survives stomach acid. Colonizes the gut. Restores beneficial bacterial diversity.
02
Digestive Enzymes
Taken with meals. Breaks down food your gut can no longer process efficiently.
03
L-Glutamine
The primary fuel for intestinal cells. Repairs mucosal lining. Reduces permeability.
04
Anti-Inflammatory Nutrition Protocol
Dr. Stoler's evidence-based elimination and reintroduction framework.

Common Questions

What People in Kigali Ask

Why is gut health often worse in urban environments like Kigali? +
Urban stress, processed food availability, and environmental toxins all deplete the microbiome. Cities are hard on gut health.
How is this different from taking a regular probiotic? +
Most retail probiotics do not survive stomach acid. This protocol uses spore-based strains proven to colonize, plus the repair and enzyme layers most people miss.
How long does gut healing take? +
Initial symptom relief typically within 7–14 days. Mucosal repair and microbiome rebalancing: 30–90 days.
Do I need testing first? +
No. The protocol is designed to work without testing. Optional advanced testing (Viome, Function Health) available through BLEU for deeper insight.
